rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Reginald Hindleys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Judgment Against Petitioners Sametc, Llc, And John Sollner And Their Attorney Of Record, Mark Epstein, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 21, 2020 line 1. RESPONDENT REGINALD HINDLEY's Motion To Enforce Judgment Against Petitioners Sametc, Llc, And John Sollner And Their Attorney Of Record, Mark Epstein, Etc. and for OSC RE: CONTEMPT OFF CALENDAR. Motions to Enforce existing judgment are heard in Department 514. Therefore, the motion to the extent it seeks to enforce the judgment, is off calendar for department 501. The motion further seeks an issuance of an OSC Re: Contempt. Authority cited in Opposition regarding the procedure for setting an OSC on a judgment is not addressed in reply. Furthermore, based on the reply, the issuance of an OSC is not yet ripe. Request to set an OSC Re: Contempt for hearing is denied without prejudice to be brought in a proper format at an appropriate time. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition By Reginald Hindley To Modify Or Vacate Arbitration Award Of October 15, 2019
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 21, 2020 line 3. PETITIONER REGINALD HINDLEY'S Hearing On Amended Petition To Modify Or Vacate Arbitration Award Of October 15, 2019 continued to October 30, 2020. By no later than September 4, 2020 Epstein parties to file an appropriate post-judgment motion in case 513720 to seek a determination/clarification as to the terms of the Judgement and its continued validity. Each party may file a supplemental brief in this action no later than October 26, 2020 addressing any changes arising out of the ruling on the motion in case 513720. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 21, 2020 line 4. Motion For Summary Judgment Hearing Required why late-filed opposition should not be stricken. According to the records on file the motion for summary judgment was originally served on May 22, 2020. It was pending until June 15, 2020 on which date it was taken off calendar by the Court due to COVID-19 pandemic with instructions to be re-set. The Motion was re-set for hearing on June 24, 2020 for a hearing date of August 21, 2020 by June 24, 2020 order setting the deadlines for the opposition and reply. Defendant's counsel appears to have been aware of the order and the due date for opposition. Record does not indicate any attempt by the defense counsel to seek an extension for the opposition set by June 24, 2020 Order. Opposition was filed late without leave of Court and in violation of June 2020 Order 7 days late. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DAMIEN QUINN VS. BELLROSE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 21, 2020 line 5. PLAINTIFF DAMIEN QUINN's DEMURRER to 4TH Amended CROSS COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR, demurrer is not accompanied by meet and confer declaration. Parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith by no later than August 25, 2020. If parties do not come on agreement, Plaintiff may re-file the demurrer, if necessary and proper, by no later than August 26, 2020 with a hearing date of September 22, 2020.= (501/CFH)
